ranked_discoveries.finding: Orphan drugs 49.2%F vs common drugs 74.5%F — 25.3pp gap (p=4.4e-82). Anti-regression absent in rare drugs.
30pp range across 16 SOCs: Skin 84.7%F to Cardiac 54.6%F. Vascular shows REVERSE anti-regression (rho=-0.733).
Strongest + most-reported signals are 96%F. Two axes (strength + volume) jointly predict female bias.
Estrogen receptor 95.5%F to Androgen receptor 5.8%F — 90pp range across drug targets.
Anti-regression holds at ALL thresholds up to 500 reports (rho>=0.964). Bootstrap CI for %F: 57.93-58.21%.
75% of individual drugs (15/20) show within-drug anti-regression. 15 are significant.
Newest drugs (2020+) least female-biased at 67.2%F. 2000s biologics peak at 79.7%F.
CRP increased 82.6%F vs CRP decreased 36.1%F — 46.5pp delta. Inflammatory biomarkers sexually dimorphic.
Leuprorelin: 23.6%F but 70% female-direction signals. Enzalutamide: 1.1%F. ED drugs 52.7%F (PAH use).
R-CHOP 38.3pp spread, ICI combo only 1.4pp. HIV ART 22.8pp. Combo drugs can diverge significantly.
83% of co-prescribed pairs are concordant (<10pp). Mean AE correlation rho=0.478.
SC injectable 81.6%F highest, inhaled 61.2%F lowest (H=407.78, p=6.25e-86).
Immune targets 85.0%F, enzyme inhibitors 83.3%F. Transporters 56.1%F most balanced.
Pediatric 43.7%F (male-skewed), elderly 87.6%F drugs. Life-stage modulates sex bias.
53.8% female-higher vs 46.2% male-higher. Female signals stronger (|LR| 1.007 vs 0.963, p=2.80e-41).
9/11 narrow therapeutic index drugs warrant sex-specific monitoring. MTX: 106 strong signals.
Rash 89.2%F across 301 drugs. Death 44.2%F. Network density 0.87%.
Tocilizumab 91.4%F (508 AEs), Risperidone 28.5%F (518 AEs). Most connected drugs are female-biased.
